name: azure-cosmos-java
description: Azure Cosmos DB SDK for Java. NoSQL database operations with global distribution, multi-model support, and reactive patterns.
capabilities: Generate Java code for Cosmos DB client initialization with key-based authentication
Create database and container operations with proper partition key configuration
Implement CRUD operations using sync and async client patterns
Write SQL queries for document retrieval with parameterized queries
Configure consistency levels and connection modes for optimal performance
Handle CosmosException errors with appropriate retry logic
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing documentation and code examples for Azure Cosmos DB Java SDK. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 findings and a risk score of 0/100. The skill provides instructional content for database operations without any executable code. The only risk factor is env_access for environment variable usage in authentication examples, which is standard practice for credential management.
valueStatement: Integrating Azure Cosmos DB into Java applications requires understanding SDK patterns and best practices. This skill provides comprehensive guidance for database operations, reactive programming, and global distribution.
userTitle: Build Cosmos DB Applications with Java
limitations: Requires valid Azure Cosmos DB account credentials for actual operations
Code examples target azure-cosmos SDK v4+ and may not work with older versions
Does not cover other Cosmos DB APIs (MongoDB, Cassandra, Gremlin, Table)
Performance tuning requires understanding of your specific workload patterns

useCases.title: Enterprise Application Developer
useCases.description: Build scalable backend services that store and retrieve data from globally distributed Cosmos DB containers with proper partitioning strategies.

useCases.title: Cloud Migration Specialist
useCases.description: Migrate existing database applications to Azure Cosmos DB with guidance on SDK integration and consistency model selection.

useCases.title: Full-Stack Developer
useCases.description: Implement reactive data access patterns using the async client for high-throughput scenarios in modern web applications.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Client Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a CosmosClient in Java using environment variables for the endpoint and key. Include proper error handling.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner setting up initial connection to Cosmos DB

promptTemplates.title: Container Creation with Partition Key
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate Java code to create a database and container in Cosmos DB. The container should use /userId as the partition key. Use the async client pattern.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating database schema for user data storage

promptTemplates.title: CRUD Operations with Reactive Pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Java code that creates, reads, updates, and deletes a user document in Cosmos DB using reactive chains with the async client. Include proper error handling for each operation.

promptTemplates.title: Optimized Query with Request Options
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a parameterized SQL query in Java that retrieves users by status from Cosmos DB. Configure CosmosQueryRequestOptions for optimal performance and iterate through paged results.

bestPractices: Reuse CosmosClient instance throughout your application - creating new clients is expensive
Choose partition keys with high cardinality and even distribution for optimal scaling
Use async client for high-throughput scenarios and reactive programming patterns
antiPatterns: Creating new CosmosClient instances for each operation - causes connection pool exhaustion
Using strong consistency level without understanding latency implications across regions
Ignoring 429 rate limit responses - always implement retry logic or use built-in retry policy

faq.question: What is the difference between CosmosClient and CosmosAsyncClient?
faq.answer: CosmosClient is synchronous and blocks until operations complete. CosmosAsyncClient uses reactive programming with non-blocking operations, better for high-throughput scenarios.

faq.question: How do I handle authentication for Cosmos DB in Java?
faq.answer: Use key-based authentication with endpoint and key from environment variables. Store credentials securely and never hardcode them in source code.

faq.question: What consistency level should I choose for my application?
faq.answer: Session consistency is recommended for most applications. Strong consistency has higher latency. Eventual consistency offers lowest latency but no ordering guarantees.

faq.question: How do I optimize query performance in Cosmos DB?
faq.answer: Use parameterized queries, select only needed properties, leverage partition key in WHERE clauses, and configure appropriate request options for your query patterns.

faq.question: What happens when I exceed my provisioned RU/s?
faq.answer: Cosmos DB returns 429 status code with retry-after header. The SDK has built-in retry logic, but you should also handle this in your application code.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for other Cosmos DB APIs besides NoSQL?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on the NoSQL (SQL) API. MongoDB, Cassandra, Gremlin, and Table APIs have different SDKs and connection patterns.
